schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

mainEntity.name: Is ESOP valuation mandatory under Indian law?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Ind AS 102 requires fair value measurement of all share-based payments at grant date. For listed companies, SEBI SBEB Regulations additionally mandate independent valuation. For tax purposes, Section 17(2)(vi) requires FMV determination at exercise.

mainEntity.name: Which model is better for ESOPs — Black-Scholes or Binomial?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on option features. Black-Scholes suits European-style options with simple vesting. Binomial Lattice is preferred for American-style options with early exercise features, complex vesting conditions, or performance hurdles. We select based on your ESOP scheme terms.

mainEntity.name: How do you estimate volatility for unlisted companies?

acceptedAnswer.text: We use comparable listed peer company volatilities, adjusted for size premium, industry beta, and capital structure leverage differences. Typically 3–5 peers with daily return data over 3–5 years matching the expected option life.

mainEntity.name: How often should ESOP valuation be updated?

acceptedAnswer.text: At minimum: at each grant date (mandatory), and annually for Ind AS 102 expense computation. We recommend quarterly updates if material new grants occur or if the underlying share value changes significantly.

mainEntity.name: Can you value ESOPs for startups with no revenue?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. For pre-revenue startups, the underlying share value is determined first using appropriate methods (Berkus, scorecard, or backsolve from latest funding round), then the option value is derived using that share value as input to the pricing model.
